advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the single-datapoint (evaluation_periods=1) firing on the data-disk alarm is intended vs the sibling root-disk alarm's 2-of-3 debounce; add datapoints_to_alarm if flapping is a concern.
evidence: infra/terraform/monitoring.tf:230-233: `period = 300` / `evaluation_periods = 1` / `threshold = 85` / `comparison_operator = "GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold"` (no datapoints_to_alarm)

monitoring_autodeploy.tf:100-104 sibling root-disk alarm uses `period=300` / `evaluation_periods=3` / `datapoints_to_alarm=2` with comment 'pages within ~15 min of sustained pressure without paging on one anomalous sample'
ADSENCE: no datapoints_to_alarm on the new alarm, so one over-threshold sample trips it
infra/terraform/monitoring.tf:225-228: `dimensions = { InstanceId = data.aws_instance.gerrit.id, mount = "/var/gerrit" }`
infra/scripts/observability.sh:24: `DATA_MOUNT="${DATA_MOUNT:-/var/gerrit}"`
infra/scripts/observability.sh:66: `--dimensions InstanceId="$IID",mount="$DATA_MOUNT"` (dimension value comes from the overridable env var, not a literal)
advisory.finding: The new data-disk alarm pages on a single 5-minute datapoint (evaluation_periods=1, no datapoints_to_alarm), unlike its sibling root-disk alarm which debounces with evaluation_periods=3 / datapoints_to_alarm=2. A single anomalous df sample at >=85% will page, and it can flap in/out of ALARM around the threshold.
merged_from: The alarm hardcodes the `mount = "/var/gerrit"` dimension, but the probe publishes disk_used_percent with mount taken from an overridable env var (DATA_MOUNT default /var/gerrit). If DATA_MOUNT is ever overridden on the host, the published dimension no longer matches the alarm's fixed dimension and the alarm silently stops receiving datapoints (sitting in INSUFFICIENT_DATA / notBreaching).
scenarios: A transient df spike (e.g. a large temporary pack file during gc) briefly reads >=85% and immediately pages, then recovers

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the pre-existing aws_instance.gerrit user_data drift is applied/triaged (or the check is non-blocking) so the terraform-drift workflow is not red on main from its first run.
evidence: .github/workflows/terraform-drift.yml:52: `run: terraform plan -input=false -lock=false -detailed-exitcode` (exit 2 on any diff fails the check)

ticket c7d4 comment: "The '1 to change' is PRE-EXISTING drift NOT authored here: aws_instance.gerrit user_data ... a genuine dc3f-class un-applied drift that the new terraform-drift CI (AC2b) would correctly flag"
.github/workflows/terraform-drift.yml:12-13: `push: branches: [main]` and `schedule: - cron: '17 6 * * *'`
advisory.finding: The drift workflow runs `terraform plan -detailed-exitcode` on push-to-main and on a daily schedule; per the ticket's own pre-merge plan there is a pre-existing, unrelated user_data drift on aws_instance.gerrit, so the check will be RED on main from the first scheduled/push run until that separate drift is applied. This is intended drift-detection behavior but means the new required-ish check is non-green immediately post-merge.
scenarios: First scheduled/push-to-main run of the workflow exits 2 and fails because of the unrelated instance user_data drift, not this ticket's resources

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Sequence the rebar-terraform-plan role creation before enabling the push:main drift trigger (or gate the workflow until the role exists) so the first post-merge run does not fail by construction.
evidence: .github/workflows/terraform-drift.yml:15-17: `push:` `branches: [main]` `paths: ['infra/terraform/**']`

iam.tf role is created only by the post-merge operator step per ticket c7d4: "terraform import ... then terraform apply to wire the alarm actions and create the role; the terraform-drift CI then reports green"
role-to-assume arn:aws:iam::896586841071:role/rebar-terraform-plan (.github/workflows/terraform-drift.yml:38) references a role that does not exist until after apply
advisory.finding: The terraform-drift workflow triggers on push to main touching infra/terraform/**, but the IAM role rebar-terraform-plan and the imported alarm it depends on only come into existence via a MANUAL post-merge `terraform import`+`apply`. The first CI run(s) after this merge will fail — either OIDC AssumeRole against a non-existent role ARN, or a non-empty plan (exit 2) — until an operator manually applies.
scenarios: This change merges; the push-to-main run of terraform-drift fires before any operator has run import/apply and fails the check.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve or explicitly triage the pre-existing aws_instance.gerrit user_data drift before enabling the push:main drift gate, and make the post-merge apply -target guard un-missable (script it) to avoid mutating the live instance.
evidence: ticket c7d4 comment: "Plan: 4 to add, 1 to change, 0 to destroy ... The '1 to change' is PRE-EXISTING drift NOT authored here: aws_instance.gerrit user_data 676ec059->1cfc59bd"
ticket c7d4 comment: "the post-merge apply of THIS ticket's resources must be TARGETED ... so it does NOT sweep in the unrelated instance user_data change"
.github/workflows/terraform-drift.yml:53: `terraform plan -input=false -lock=false -detailed-exitcode` (exit 2 on the pre-existing diff -> failing check on main)
advisory.finding: The push:main daily drift sweep will report RED on main due to a pre-existing, unrelated aws_instance.gerrit user_data drift (676ec059->1cfc59bd) that this change does not author and the CI cannot remediate; correspondingly the ticket's post-merge `terraform apply` must be manually `-target`ed, because an untargeted apply would sweep that unreviewed user_data change onto the live Gerrit instance.
scenarios: Daily schedule / push-to-main runs exit 2 on the unrelated instance drift, keeping the gate red.
An operator runs `terraform apply` without -target and mutates the live Gerrit instance's user_data (potential replacement/reboot blast radius) as a side effect of adopting the alarm.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Narrow the OIDC sub condition to the specific ref(s)/environment that run terraform-drift (e.g. repo:navapbc/rebar:ref:refs/heads/main and pull_request) rather than repo:navapbc/rebar:*.
evidence: iam.tf (diff): variable `token.actions.githubusercontent.com:sub`, values `["repo:navapbc/rebar:*"]` with test `StringLike`
comment in diff: "Scope to this repo (any ref/branch/PR). Narrow further with a specific ref if the drift job is ever pinned to one branch."
advisory.finding: The OIDC trust policy scopes the sub condition to repo:navapbc/rebar:* (StringLike), allowing any branch, tag, or pull-request ref of the repo to assume rebar-terraform-plan; it is not pinned to a ref/environment.
scenarios: A workflow on any branch/PR of the repo (or a compromised workflow file on a feature branch) can assume the read-only plan role and enumerate account-wide resources.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider replacing account-wide ReadOnlyAccess with a scoped describe/list policy for the services this stack plans (EC2/EBS/SNS/CloudWatch/SSM/IAM/DLM/S3-state) to reduce the read blast radius.
evidence: iam.tf (diff): `policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess"`
ticket c7d4 advisory triage: "ReadOnlyAccess is broader than a hand-rolled describe/list policy — ACCEPTED"
advisory.finding: The role attaches AWS-managed ReadOnlyAccess, granting account-wide read (including secret metadata across all services) far beyond the describe/list surface a terraform plan of this stack requires.
scenarios: The plan-only role can read any resource/secret metadata in account 896586841071, widening blast radius if the OIDC assumption is ever abused.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Scope `rebar-terraform-plan` SSM/KMS access to only `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` (or the specific params/keys the plan reads) instead of ReadOnlyAccess-wide ssm:GetParameter + kms:Decrypt on resources=["*"].
evidence: iam.tf:176 `policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess"` (AWS-managed ReadOnlyAccess includes ssm:GetParameter/GetParameters/GetParametersByPath account-wide)
iam.tf:183-190 `actions = ["kms:Decrypt"]` with `resources = ["*"]` and only `kms:ViaService = ssm.<region>` as constraint — not scoped to a specific key or the /rebar/prod/* param namespace

ssm.tf:17-50 the SSM namespace this role can decrypt holds real secrets: /rebar/prod/anthropic-api-key, github-oauth-client-secret, g2p-github-pat, ci-gerrit-ssh-key, reviewbot-tickets-pat, gerrit-admin-password
monitoring.tf:31-34 the plan's only actual SecureString read is `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` — the grant is broader than the stated need
advisory.finding: The `rebar-terraform-plan` OIDC role's KMS inline statement grants `kms:Decrypt` on `resources = ["*"]` constrained only by `kms:ViaService=ssm.<region>`, and its attached AWS-managed `ReadOnlyAccess` grants account-wide `ssm:GetParameter*`. Together the role can read and decrypt EVERY `/rebar/prod/*` SecureString (anthropic-api-key, github-oauth-client-secret, g2p-github-pat, ci-gerrit-ssh-key, reviewbot-tickets-pat, gerrit-admin-password, etc.), far beyond the single `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` value the plan actually reads.
scenarios: Any principal/job able to assume rebar-terraform-plan can GetParameter+Decrypt every prod secret, not just the alert endpoint.
advisory.suggested_fix: Replace ReadOnlyAccess with a hand-scoped read policy (describe/list for the resources a plan reads) plus `ssm:GetParameter` and `kms:Decrypt` limited by resource ARN to `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` and its backing key.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Narrow the OIDC trust `sub` to a specific branch/ref (e.g. `repo:navapbc/rebar:ref:refs/heads/main`) and/or remove the untrusted-ref `pull_request` plan path so attacker-controlled HCL cannot run under the secret-reading role.
evidence: .github/workflows/terraform-drift.yml:13-14 `pull_request:` with `paths: ['infra/terraform/**']`
iam.tf:156-160 trust `sub` = StringLike `repo:navapbc/rebar:*` — matches any branch/PR ref, per the code comment 'Scope to this repo (any ref/branch/PR)'

terraform-drift.yml:50-53 `terraform plan` runs against the checked-out ref, which for a PR is the PR HEAD's HCL — a plan evaluates data sources (e.g. `data aws_ssm_parameter { with_decryption = true }`) that an attacker can add

MITIGATION EVIDENCE: .github/workflows/lockdown.yml:1-9 GitHub is a read-only mirror and PRs are auto-closed (real intake is Gerrit); GitHub also does not issue OIDC id-token to fork-PR workflows by default — this narrows the surface to same-repo branch refs, but does not eliminate it
advisory.finding: The `terraform-drift` workflow runs `terraform plan` on `pull_request` events for infra/terraform/**, and the role's trust policy `sub` condition is `StringLike repo:navapbc/rebar:*` (any branch/PR ref). `terraform plan` executes the checked-out ref's HCL/data-sources; a PR-controlled change can add a decrypting SSM/external data source, so untrusted PR-supplied HCL reaches an account-wide secret-read+decrypt sink.
scenarios: A same-repo branch/PR touching infra/terraform/** adds a decrypting data source; the drift plan reads and logs/exfiltrates prod secrets under the OIDC role.
advisory.suggested_fix: Pin the trust condition to `repo:navapbc/rebar:ref:refs/heads/main` (and workflow_dispatch/schedule which run trusted refs), and drop `pull_request` from the credential-bearing job or gate it to non-decrypting operation.
